dialogue.utterance: _DISPATCH: Unit 3, respond to McDonald's on 9th and Moseley for a patient with possible seizure. She is ambulatory.
Hi, my name is Casey, and I'm an EMT with the rescue squad. What caused you to call 911 today?
I feel like I am going to have a seizure again. I felt shaky at work, and my left hand was numb.
Can you tell me your name and your birthday?
What city are we in right now?
What is today's date and time approximately?
Can you describe what happened today that led you to call 911?
Were you able to keep your eyes open when you came into this area?
When I asked about your symptoms, did you answer clearly?
When you were asked to open your hands, did you respond without being told again?
You were able to move without needing repetition.
Have you had this type of symptom before? Any medical conditions or prior episodes?
I have a "hole in my heart" and had seizures as a child. I haven't had one since, but felt like I was going to today at work.
Do you have any known allergies to medications or other substances?
Yes, I have an allergy to amoxicillin. I was told before and had a bad reaction.
On a scale of 0 to 10, how would you rate your symptoms of shakiness and numbness in your left hand?
Zero-I don't have pain-but I am very shaky.
Are you having any trouble breathing or speaking?
Please stay still as I check your breathing.
Let me feel your pulse to check for regularity and strength.
I will now look for signs of rashes, bruises, or bite marks on your skin.
I'm checking for signs of swelling or stiffness in your neck.
Can you open and close your jaw for me to assess movement?
You appear alert with no signs of neck swelling or stiffness.
Your nose and ears look normal. Any pain or drainage?
Your mouth is normal with no ulcers or sores.
I'll place pads on your chest for a quick look at your heart rhythm.
Your pulse was 78 initially and has now dropped to 66. It is regular.
Your respiratory rate was 11 then increased to 28-still okay.
Your blood pressure was 164 over 71 and now is 132 over 74.
Your initial blood sugar was 108 mg/dL, then read zero. The second may not be accurate.
Your oxygen saturation was 99% and now is at 98%-still fine.
ECG shows a right bundle branch block with a widened QRS. I'll run a 12-lead.
Your initial respiratory rate was 11. No distress noted.
Let me now perform a 12-lead EKG to confirm findings. It'll take a moment.
I'll place you on ECG monitoring. It should help us watch your heart. Do you agree?
Skin, head, neck, ears, and thorax exams show everything normal and within limits.
After reviewing your situation, we'll follow the medical-seizure protocol and continue monitoring during transport.
Your pulse is steady at 66. Would you like to answer more questions while we check?
Your breathing is at 28 and not evident of distress or difficulty.
Blood pressure is still 132 over 74, elevated but not alarmingly so.
Oxygen saturation remains at 98. Let me check the EKG once more.
12-lead shows a right bundle branch block with no ST-segment changes or other abnormalities.
Sinus rhythm found with a bundle branch block-otherwise heart looks stable.
You mentioned the numbness and shakiness. Have you had other symptoms to report?
Does the numbness worsen with movement or certain positions?
Is it like pins and needles or a complete lack of feeling in your left hand?
Does the feeling stay in your hand or move up your arm or neck?
Even with a zero pain rating, does the numbness cause anxiety or discomfort?
How long had your symptoms existed before you called 911?
You're managing your blood pressure with labetalol. How do you take it, and is it working well?
Were there other allergies or medications we need to note?
You're not on seizure medications due to lack of insurance. Any other treatment gaps?
No active airway signs, and you are able to speak clearly and continuously.
You had seizures as a child. Any family history or known heart history with that?
What was your last meal or drink before symptoms started?
You mentioned it happened at work. Was anything like stress or a specific task involved?
We're avoiding an IV for now given reports you are hard to start. It'll be attempted at the hospital.
We will head to [COMPANY_NAME] Emergency Department 28 for continuing care.
Please stay still when we move you into the ambulance.
No signs of recent injuries, burns, or rashes on your body.
I'll now check for signs of injury or deformity in your arms and legs.
No deformities or swelling found on your arms and legs-strength appears normal.
The numbness in your left hand doesn't seem to be related to physical limb changes.
Let me quickly assess your abdomen for tenderness or pain.
Your belly is flat, without pain or rigidity-normal bowel sounds heard.
The EKG currently reads a right bundle branch block-no other changes.
Just to confirm-do you know where you are and why you are here right now?
You are alert and oriented to person, place, time, and event (A&Ox4).
Glasgow Coma Scale of 15-full consciousness.
Securing you on the cot for safe transfer to the ambulance.
IV access is deferred at this time-will be attempted at the hospital.
You're not currently experiencing chest pain or discomfort.
[COMPANY_NAME] has experience with unusual heart rhythms and is well-suited for you.
You are breathing shallow but equal on both sides without signs of trouble.
Limbs are warm and no cyanosis detected.
Rechecking, oxygen remains at 98%-no change in reading.
Oxygen and pulse readings remain consistent with no fluctuation.
You started symptoms at work. When exactly, and how sudden was it?
These symptoms seem more mental-was that your impression as well?
Tell me again what kind of reaction you had to amoxicillin in the past?
How long ago your seizure and what was the diagnosis back then?
We'll do a final check of vitals during transition into the ambulance.
Let me get latest blood pressure readings one more time.
Pulse went from 66 to 68 now-still normal.
Zero might not be right. Could it be an issue with the finger position or dryness?
Your breathing went from 28 to 26-still okay. Any increase in labor or discomfort?
EKG has stayed consistent with initial and second recordings.
You reported numbness-any heat, cold, or weakness with it?
Was the numbness constant or came and went since it began?
Would breakfast be your normal period for eating, and did you eat today?
Just checking your Glasgow score-you score a 15, showing you are A&Ox4.
Skin turgor is normal and no jaundice. Temperature appears normal and not hot.
Airway is patent and maintains itself naturally with no need for support.
Let me know immediately if you feel anything uncomfortable while we transfer.
We continue on protocol 3-12 for seizure with ECG monitoring. ED is aware.
I'll secure your head now. Feel free to ask for any comfort needed.
All patches and monitoring are ready for travel with no loose equipment.
Please remain upright until the vehicle stops so we can move safely.
I have you secured and you may now lie back on the cot.
Let me check your restraints and that your oxygen is still running fine.
You look comfortable and I'll begin the transport now.
IV access will be completed at the hospital under their protocol.
All ECG actions passed to receiving nurse-update given in handoff.
How do you prefer to be contacted by hospital for any further follow-up?
Doors are open now and I'll start the transfer while still observing for any changes.
We're arriving and will be offloading soon. Stable condition.
No IV repeated. Pressure dressings remain and handed over to hospital.
Anyone expecting you at the hospital? Shall we contact a family member?
Got you safely over. Wait for ED before anyone assists you further.
Handing off all findings and care during transport to nurse and doctor.
All steps have been shared with receiving physician and nurse at ED.
